pullRequest.title: fix: handle multi-byte UTF-8 chars in SQL special char detection
pullRequest.body:
## what

- Extract multi-byte UTF-8 characters (´ U+00B4, ' U+2018, ' U+2019) from regex character classes into alternations to prevent byte-by-byte matching that caused false positives with non-Latin scripts (Chinese, Japanese, Arabic, Korean, Hebrew)
- Affects rules: 942420, 942421, 942430, 942431, 942432
- Creates shared `regex-assembly/include/sql-special-chars-anomaly.ra` include file and composable `.ra` files for all 5 rules using named assemblies

## Test plan
- [x] All 52 regression tests pass across 5 rules (942420, 942421, 942430, 942431, 942432)
- [x] `crs-toolchain regex compare` confirms generated regex matches for all 5 rules
- [x] Tests cover: ASCII special chars, multi-byte UTF-8 special chars (´ ' '), and negative tests for non-Latin scripts (Chinese, Japanese, Arabic, Korean, Hebrew)
- [x] CI passes

## refs

- Fixes #3325

pullRequest.body: Fixes #3498

pullRequest.title: fix(933150): moving printf to 933160 for additional php syntax check (933150 PL-1, 933160 PL-1)
pullRequest.body:
Fixes a false positive with ``printf`` matching ``SprintForTheCause`` by moving it to 933160 which checks for additional PHP syntax instead of just ``printf``.

closes #3641

pullRequest.title: fix: create a stricter sibling to 932370 and move `at` to PL-2 (932370 PL-1, 932371 PL-2)
pullRequest.body:
This PR fixes false positive with newline characters preceding the Windows `at` command (Not UNIX) by creating a stricter sibling at PL-2 to detect `at` commands. The only other way to fix this false positive is by removing newline characters from the evasion prefix, however newline characters have been used for evasions in the past.

closes #3953

pullRequest.title: fix: fix linting of releases
pullRequest.body:
Improve the detection of the version that the linter should use to check the `ver` tags. We now check for:
- a version in the commit message: "...release...vx.y.z..."
- a version in the branch name: ".../release/vx.y.z"
- fall back to looking at the latest tag, as before

Fixes #4227

pullRequest.title: fix: add separate rule to match unix commands with no arguments
pullRequest.body:
A previous PR resolved false positives relating to invalid commands being blocked (Which would match natural english words, session cookies, uuids, and similar) in #3735 by adding the `@` processor to all commands less than 5 characters. The `@` processor was modified to match on commands with no arguments to avoid false negatives, this also meant that some commands needed to be excluded to reduce false positives because commands were being blocked with no arguments.

This PR solves both problems by creating a dedicated rule for blocking commands with no arguments, reverting the `@` processor to it's initial behavior and only block the handful of commands that can be actually executed with no arguments. This helps keeps false positives to a minimum.

False positives are further reduced by having a stricter sibling at PL-3, a handful of commands are excluding at PL-2 and lower due to the command names overlapping with common parameter names and values (i.e id, pwd, uname, etc).

closes: #4241
closes: #4388

pullRequest.title: feat: update restricted files and file extensions
pullRequest.body:
This PR was compiled from multiple sources:
- Nikto Scan
- Files commonly accessed by bots
- CrowdSec's Sec Lists (https://github.com/crowdsecurity/sec-lists/blob/master/web/sensitive_data.txt, https://github.com/crowdsecurity/sec-lists/blob/master/web/path_traversal.txt)
- https://github.com/github/gitignore
- Sensitive files I personally found
- GitLab omnibus config files

I've removed entries that were not relevant for web applications to keep false positives to a minimum, and commented out ones that cause false positives.

These rules should be overhauled in the medium to long term, but this should be fine for now. I've opened an issue in #4300 to discuss further improvements to these rules.

closes #4248

pullRequest.body: This PR solves #4251 by adding the Expect header to the list of forbidden headers: `tx.restricted_headers_basic`.

This PR adds a chained rule to rule 932180 to reduce false positive with substring matches. It checks for entries in `restricted-upload.data` that commonly result in false positives and in effect enforces a word boundary for those keywords if they exist within the parameter.

closes #4320

pullRequest.title: fix(956110): move rule to pl-2
pullRequest.body:
## Proposed changes

956110 is intended to match Ruby ERB templates within response bodies to detect data leakage, however this syntax also overlaps with some common JavaScript templating engines (Zabbix, WordPress, and Horde IMP). I don't see an way to modify this rule to prevent these false positives since the syntax is virtually identical so I'm moving this to level 2.

Closes #4343

pullRequest.title: feat: resolve common false positives with ad and tracker cookies

## Proposed changes

This PR resolves false positives with the new Google tracking cookies and a few other common tracking/ad cookies. I've moved the existing cookie exceptions to it's own file `REQUEST-999-COMMON-EXCEPTIONS-AFTER.conf` to improve the overall maintainability of these exceptions. The `999` in the filename is to ensure that the file is loaded after all the request rules have been created.


closes https://github.com/coreruleset/coreruleset/issues/4370

pullRequest.title: fix: restrict scheduled workflows to main repository
pullRequest.body:
## Proposed changes

Scheduled workflows (`duty-scheduler.yaml`, `stale.yml`) run on forks and fail due to missing resources (gh-pages branch, write permissions). Added repository checks to skip execution on forks.

**Changes:**
- Added `if: github.repository == 'coreruleset/coreruleset'` to `duty-report` job in `.github/workflows/duty-scheduler.yaml`
- Added `if: github.repository == 'coreruleset/coreruleset'` to `stale` job in `.github/workflows/stale.yml`

pullRequest.title: feat(lfi): Add detection for Vite.js path traversal (CVE-2025-30208)
pullRequest.body:
## Description

Add detection for Vite.js development server path traversal vulnerability (CVE-2025-30208).

Closes #4404

### The Problem

Vite.js development servers expose `/@fs/` and `/@id/` endpoints that allow arbitrary file system access. Attackers are actively scanning production servers for these endpoints.

Current CRS rule 930130 catches *some* payloads (like `/@fs/.env`) because `.env` is in `restricted-files.data`, but does NOT catch system files like `/@fs/etc/passwd` because rule 930120 only checks `ARGS`, not `REQUEST_URI`.

### The Solution

A dedicated rule (930140) matching the `/@fs/` and `/@id/` prefixes provides:
- Complete coverage regardless of payload
- Clear CVE attribution in logs
- No false positive risk (these endpoints have no production use)

### Testing

- 5 positive tests covering various attack patterns
- 2 negative tests ensuring no false positives on legitimate URLs

### References

- https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2025-30208
- https://github.com/vitejs/vite/security/advisories/GHSA-x574-m823-4x7w

pullRequest.title: fix(934190): add new rule to check localhost variants without scheme
pullRequest.body:
## what

- added localhost and other schemeless variants
- added tests coverage for:

  Standard localhost variants:
  - localhost/, localhost.localdomain/, localhost4/, localhost4.localdomain4/, ipv6-localhost/, ip6-loopback/

  Container environment hostnames:
  - Docker: host.docker.internal/, gateway.docker.internal/, kubernetes.docker.internal/
  - Podman: host.containers.internal/
  - Kubernetes: kubernetes.default.svc.cluster.local/

  Testing services:
  - localtest.me/, lvh.me/

  Negative tests:
  - localhost without trailing slash (should NOT trigger)
  - Partial domain match like "mylocalhost.example.com" (should NOT trigger)
  - Legitimate external hostname (should NOT trigger)

## why

- catch early on some frameworks that will complete the name with the scheme

## refs

- fixes #4427

pullRequest.title: fix(933100): reduce false positive on Extensible Metadata Platform and xsl-stylesheets
pullRequest.body:
## Proposed changes

Hello,

Following this issue: https://github.com/coreruleset/coreruleset/issues/4443 , I see no reason why we should not treat this as a false positive, and I do not see any abuse or bypass use case at this time on the scope of this rule.

```
<?xpacket begin="?" id="W5M0MpCehiHzreSzNTczkc9d"?>
<x:xmpmeta xmlns:x="adobe:ns:meta/" x:xmptk="Adobe XMP Core 5.4-c002 1.000000, 0000/00/00-00:00:00        ">
```

At this point, I don’t think it would be relevant to create a custom rule to block this, but perhaps one of you may have a different opinion on the matter.

pullRequest.title: fix: don't block json variable names called `profile` on libmodsecurity3/coraza
pullRequest.body:
## Proposed changes

When sending JSON data to libModSecurity3/Coraza, variable names are prefixed with `.json`. If the variable name is called `profile`, then that will translate to `ARGS_NAMES:json.profile` which matches one of the entries inside of `lfi-os-files.data` resulting in a false positive. This PR adds a configure-time rule exclusion to resolve this false positive.

closes: https://github.com/coreruleset/coreruleset/issues/4471

## PR Checklist

<!-- _Put an `x` in the boxes that apply. You can also fill these out after creating the PR. If you're unsure about any of them, don't hesitate to ask. We're here to help! This is simply a reminder of what we are going to look for before merging your code._ -->
